reference: Hi, my husband is thirty-seven years old and got his liver and kidney tested as he is a regular alcohol consumer. All his reports are within the given range, but for serum glutamate oxaloacetate transaminase - forty, serum glutamate pyruvate transaminase - sixty-six, and gamma-glutamyl transferase - one hundred and thirteen. His cholesterol level is two hundred and thirty-nine. What does this indicate? Does he have some serious health issue?
text: Hi. My husband is 37 years old and got his liver and kidney tested, and he is a regular alcohol consumer. All his reports are within the given range, but for serum glutamate oxaloacetate transaminase: 40; serum glutamate pyruvate transaminase: 66; and gamma-glutamyl transferase: 113. His cholesterol level is 239. What does this indicate? Do you have some serious health issue?
